text:
Once in a small coastal village, they had a great baker named Clara. She’d bake marvelous pastries every morning. Everyone knew how much she loved birds. There was always one particular favorite—a glossy starling she called Nora. Whenever Nora visited, Clara would sit her on a window sill outside her bakery, singing tales of distant lands.

After many seasons, Clara passed away. Nora never flew away. Instead, every evening, she’d visit the bakery window, her voice echoing through the empty halls.

The villagers talked about Clara for years. They’d say her laughter would still carry like a gentle sea breeze. Then one quiet evening, on a night just like every other, Nora perched on the edge of the window sill, casting a silhouette into the shop’s candlelit interior.

An old boy scout, noticing her silhouette late at night, thought of Clara. He’d made many adventures, so when Nora flitted through the window, he felt a rush. He approached gently, hoping to
